elements.alt: Diagram showing elements of a prompt pointing to specific text segments.

content.text:
Generate 3-5 bullet points to prepare me for a policy 
update meeting with Agency X. Focus on Email and 
Teams chats since June. Please use simple language so 
I can get up to speed quickly.

elements.text: Summarize teams, email and channel messages from last working day

content.text: Go to Copilot in Teams or https:///www.microsoft365.com/chat

In chat box write this example prompt below

“Summarize my mails, Teams messages and channel messages from the last workday. 
List action items in a dedicated column. Suggest follow-ups if possible, in a dedicated 
column. The table should look like this: Type (Mail/Teams/Channel) | Topic | 
Summarization | Action item | Follow-up. If I have been directly mentioned, make the 
font of the topic bold”
How did it work? Provide feedback, choose either the thumbs up or down reaction

elements.text: Create a Copilot usage guide for your role

content.text:
Go to Copilot in Teams or https:///www.microsoft365.com/chat or if you don’t have an 
M365 Copilot License go to https://copilot.microsoft.com/

In chat box write this example prompt below

"Create a Microsoft Copilot usage guide tailored to my role as a [insert your job title] at 
[insert your agency or department]. Include examples of how I can use Copilot in Word, 
Excel, Outlook, Teams, and PowerPoint to improve productivity, streamline 
communication, and support public service delivery. Also suggest tips and sample 
prompts for getting started and best practices for using Copilot responsibly."
How did it work? Provide feedback, choose either the thumbs up or down reaction

sections.title: Practice : Email Summaries and Questions in Copilot Chat

content.text: Go to Copilot in Teams or https:///www.microsoft365.com/chat
In chat box write this example prompt below
“Give me a summary of emails I have received on <this topic>“
“Tell me about recent emails I have received from <this person>“
How did it work? Provide feedback, choose either the thumbs up or down reaction
Go to Copilot in Teams, Outlook or https:///www.microsoft365.com/chat

sections.title: Practice : From Copilot Chat to Outlook

content.text: “Help me write an email to <someone> about <a topic>”
Paste the email template from the previous Copilot Chat into the body of the email
Locate the options to auto rewrite or get coaching when you highlight a particulate section of text.
How did it work? Provide feedback, choose either the thumbs up or down reaction
Go to Outlook on your computer or https://outlook.office365.com/
Locate an email or an email thread

Help me come up with a response to this email. Acknowledge the email and the 
ideas everyone brought up. Keep is short and sweet.
Type an additional clarification and select the Submit icon

Then explore some of the other options, including Retry, Make it Shorter, Make it Longer, Change 
Tone. Select Keep it when you are done.
How did it work? Provide feedback, choose either the thumbs up or down reaction
Go to Outlook on your computer or https://outlook.office365.com/
Locate an email or an email thread where you have a draft email response
Select Coaching from the Copilot menu
Evaluate the tone, reader sentiment, and clarity recommendations
How did it work? Provide feedback, choose either the thumbs up or down reaction
Go to Outlook on your mobile device
Open an email or an email thread
Locate the Copilot button at the top of the app
Select the Copilot button to generate a summary of the email thread
How did it work? Provide feedback, choose either the thumbs up or down reaction
